/home/nightly/congaree-gx/2015-10-26.193953/build/install/tile/usr/lib/bme/usr/include/bme/va_alloc.h File Reference

Bare Metal Environment virtual address allocator. More...


Typedefs

typedef uint32_t bme_va_pool_t
 Handle on allocated pool.

Functions

int bme_create_va_pool (VA start_va, int page_size, int num_pages, bme_va_pool_t *va_pool)
 Create a pool of virtual address space from which virtual addresses can be allocated.
void bme_free_va_pool (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool)
 Frees the storage occupied by a virtual address pool.
int bme_alloc_va_range (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool, int num_pages, VA *va)
void bme_free_va_range (bme_va_pool_t *va_pool, int num_pages, VA va)
 Free num_pages of contiguous allocated VA range, starting at the page on which va resides.

Detailed Description

Bare Metal Environment virtual address allocator.

This functionality allows users to create pools of contiguous virtual address space. The pool must be created with a designated page size. Users are responsible for ensuring that the range of addresses covered by the pool is available for use. Once the pool is created, ranges within the pool can be allocated and freed in increments of the pool's page size.
